description: Hydropathy comparison showing lrx-1 lacks transmembrane hydrophobic segment in mature protein while CD320 has clear TM helix
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Create a hydropathy comparison figure
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(14, 8), sharex=False)
fig.suptitle('Hydropathy Analysis: lrx-1 vs Human CD320', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')

kd = {
    'A': 1.8, 'R': -4.5, 'N': -3.5, 'D': -3.5, 'C': 2.5,
    'Q': -3.5, 'E': -3.5, 'G': -0.4, 'H': -3.2, 'I': 4.5,
    'L': 3.8, 'K': -3.9, 'M': 1.9, 'F': 2.8, 'P': -1.6,
    'S': -0.8, 'T': -0.7, 'W': -0.9, 'Y': -1.3, 'V': 4.2
}

def hydro_profile(seq, window=19):
    scores = []
    for i in range(len(seq) - window + 1):
        segment = seq[i:i+window]
        score = sum(kd.get(aa, 0) for aa in segment) / window
        scores.append(score)
    return scores

lrx1_seq = ""
cd320_seq = ""

# lrx-1 hydropathy
h1 = hydro_profile(lrx1_seq)
pos1 = np.arange(1, len(h1)+1)
ax1 = axes[0]
ax1.fill_between(pos1, h1, 0, where=[x>0 for x in h1], color='#FF6666', alpha=0.5, label='Hydrophobic')
ax1.fill_between(pos1, h1, 0, where=[x<=0 for x in h1], color='#6666FF', alpha=0.5, label='Hydrophilic')
ax1.plot(pos1, h1, 'k-', linewidth=0.5)
ax1.axhline(y=1.6, color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, label='TM threshold (1.6)')
ax1.axhline(y=0, color='gray', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
# Annotate signal peptide
ax1.axvspan(1, 19, alpha=0.2, color='gold', label='Signal peptide')
ax1.set_title('C. elegans lrx-1 (Q22179, 368 aa)', fontsize=12)
ax1.set_ylabel('Hydropathy Score\n(KD, window=19)')
ax1.set_xlim(1, 370)
ax1.set_ylim(-3, 3)
ax1.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=8)
ax1.annotate('Signal peptide\n(only hydrophobic region)', xy=(10, 1.8), 
            fontsize=9, color='darkred', fontweight='bold')
ax1.annotate('NO transmembrane helix\nin mature protein', xy=(250, 1.5),
            fontsize=10, color='red', fontweight='bold',
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.3', facecolor='lightyellow', edgecolor='red'))

# CD320 hydropathy
h2 = hydro_profile(cd320_seq)
pos2 = np.arange(1, len(h2)+1)
ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.fill_between(pos2, h2, 0, where=[x>0 for x in h2], color='#FF6666', alpha=0.5, label='Hydrophobic')
ax2.fill_between(pos2, h2, 0, where=[x<=0 for x in h2], color='#6666FF', alpha=0.5, label='Hydrophilic')
ax2.plot(pos2, h2, 'k-', linewidth=0.5)
ax2.axhline(y=1.6, color='red', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, label='TM threshold (1.6)')
ax2.axhline(y=0, color='gray', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax2.axvspan(1, 35, alpha=0.2, color='gold', label='Signal peptide')
ax2.axvspan(230, 250, alpha=0.3, color='red', label='Transmembrane helix')
ax2.set_title('Human CD320 (Q9NPF0, 282 aa) - Ortholog', fontsize=12)
ax2.set_ylabel('Hydropathy Score\n(KD, window=19)')
ax2.set_xlabel('Residue Position')
ax2.set_xlim(1, 290)
ax2.set_ylim(-3, 3)
ax2.legend(loc='upper right', fontsize=8)
ax2.annotate('Transmembrane helix\n(confirmed)', xy=(240, 2.3), 
            fontsize=9, color='darkred', fontweight='bold')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('hydropathy_comparison.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Figure saved: hydropathy_comparison.png")
